vitals.terrain: Coastal flats with short climbs and summit finish

course_description.overview: The 110.8 km course starts and finishes at Cerro El Morro in Salinas, following sections of the Spondylus Route with ocean views, beaches, mangroves, fishing villages, and rural landscapes. Features flat sections, short climbs, smooth descents, and a summit finish climb of 1.6 km to Cerro El Morro with up to 12% gradients.[1][2]

key_segments.name: Spondylus Route
key_segments.description: Coastal route passing beaches, mangroves, fishing villages, and rural areas with continuous Pacific Ocean views and tropical vegetation.[2]

key_segments.name: Cerro El Morro Summit Finish
key_segments.description: Final climb with gradients up to 12%, serving as a decisive challenge with panoramic views of Salinas and the Pacific.[2]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Fun and varied rhythm with flat speed sections, short climbs for attacks, smooth descents, and a steep summit finish demanding strong legs and strategy. Medium 62 km option available with 576 m elevation.[2]

logistics.getting_there: Excellent road connectivity to Salinas, a major tourist destination in Santa Elena province.[2]
logistics.accommodation: Wide range of hotels available in Salinas, with race week activities at Salón Hotel Presidente including Pasta Fest and group ride.[1][2]
logistics.registration_details: General Ticket includes premium Italian jersey, Elite bottle, full aid stations, closed/controlled course, and plate. Long course for rankings and podium eligibility; medium course non-competitive. Register via salinas.gfny.com.[1][2]
logistics.bike_logistics: Perfect mobility with wide roads, excellent asphalt, and traffic control from start. Chip timing provided.[2]
logistics.local_considerations: Coastal setting with beaches, boardwalk, fishing port, diverse gastronomy, and warm hospitality. Vibrant local support expected.[1][2]

final_verdict.one_liner: Inaugural GFNY opener on Ecuador's stunning Pacific coast with summit finish spectacle.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking coastal adventure, qualifier spots, and GFNY rankings in a scenic, accessible setting.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unique ocean views, varied course, and season kickoff value.
pro_tips: Opt for long course to qualify for NYC Worlds top 10% corral.
Preview area on Saturday group ride.
Enjoy post-race celebrations at El Morro summit.
